title: Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data | Stats NZ
content:
## Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data
We know your information is important to you. That’s why it’s important for us to look after it.
We have high standards for keeping your information private, secure, and confidential. We’re trusted as data leaders to set the standard for collecting and using data.
The information we collect is primarily used by Stats NZ and trusted researchers to produce statistics and research to benefit New Zealand.

## How Stats NZ keeps your data safe
This animation shows what we collect, what we do with your data, and how we are committed to protecting it.
Read the transcript of this video (PDF, 54.8KB)

## We protect your identity
We do not identify individuals in published statistics or research.
We may release some information about businesses in line with the Data and Statistics Act 2022. This means releasing selected business data to other organisations to produce and improve official statistics of national importance. Business Register release policy has more information.

## Laws and standards keep your data safe
Your information is protected by legislation and by the safeguards we have put in place. You can read more about our ‘Five Safes’ and 'Nga Tikanga Paihere' frameworks on How we keep integrated data safe Stats NZ
We apply ethical, statistical, and security best-practice standards to the data we collect. Others who use the data must apply the same standards.
All Stats NZ staff, especially those who collect data, undergo Ministry of Justice criminal conviction check before they are employed by us. If they have worked in the public sector within the last three years, we also complete a Serious Misconduct check with their previous agency(s).
All Stats NZ staff and researchers who use the information we hold sign a confidentiality certificate. They must sign it before they can work with data. The declaration is a lifetime agreement to keep data confidential.
- privacy by collecting only the information we need to produce statistics and research
- security by keeping data safe from unauthorised access and use
- confidentiality by not releasing information that could identify individuals, households, or businesses
- transparency in all our processes. We work with the Office of the Privacy Commissioner to make sure we follow best practice.

## We store your data securely
We keep information for as long as it has statistical or research value.
Once we process the data, we store it securely on our network. Our IT team monitors and evaluates security issues and risks.
We are required to retain the data collected for a minimum of 12 months. After this time, if the data is no longer needed, we securely destroy it.

## Call Recording Statement
To ensure we provide the best possible service, and for quality improvement purposes, we record all our inbound and outbound calls to our Contact Centre .
The purposes of recording these calls are to:
- help with staff training and development to ensure we provide you with consistent and accurate service
- monitor and improve the quality of our customer service
- have an accurate record of the conversation, which may be needed to support any questions or feedback
- allow us to track the types and numbers of enquiries we receive so we can continue to improve our services.

## Privacy and Security
We understand your personal information is important, and we are committed to protecting it.
- All call recordings are treated as confidential information and are stored securely
- We only retain your personal information, including call recordings, for as long as necessary to fulfil the purposes above and to comply with our legal obligations
- Access to the recordings is strictly controlled and limited to authorised personnel
- You may request a copy of your recorded call by emailing info@stats.govt .nz
- Recordings are destroyed securely after 12 months or when they are no longer needed.
If you have any questions about this statement or how we handle your information, please contact : 0800 809 464 complaints@stats.govt.nz

## Data is linked to make it more useful
We link the information people give us with information from other agencies. This makes the information more useful to researchers who are answering important questions about New Zealand.
This linked, or integrated data is held in our research databases. The Integrated Data Infrastructure (IDI) holds microdata about people and households. The Longitudinal Business Database (LBD) holds microdata about businesses.
Trusted researchers may be allowed to access data we hold. Access to data is controlled and secure. We check all research and statistics produced to make sure confidentiality is maintained.

## Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) to analyse feedback
We sometimes ask people to share their feedback about their experience taking part in our business and household surveys. To help analyse this feedback, we are using an Artificial Intelligence (AI) tool to identify themes, trends, and opportunities to make improvements.
Processes are in place to ensure that all information that directly identifies survey participants, such as contact details and reference information, is removed before feedback is analysed. This includes any personal information provided in free-text boxes.
AI-assisted analysis is only used to generate insights, and does not make any decisions without human involvement and oversight. All analysis is carried out within Stats NZ’s secure systems.
Stats NZ is committed to the responsible and ethical use of AI. If you have any questions about the way we manage your information, or would like to know more about how we’re using AI, contact us using the details below.

## Request a copy of your information
You have the right to ask for a copy of any personal information we hold about you, and to ask for it to be corrected.
To request a copy of your personal information, contact our Information Centre: 0508 525 525 (toll-free) info@stats.govt.nz
Page updated 20 May 2026
